paper_process.narrative: Stage is Qualified, 43 days from a 21 Aug 2026 close, with no MSA/SOW/procurement-onboarding activity on record anywhere. This is expected this early in the motion, but it means there is currently no contracting forcing function behind the close date.

decision_process.narrative: No committee structure, approval chain, or internal Intuit process description appears in any call or Next Step entry. Six named Intuit stakeholders sit on the Contact Role list (Chontos, Mathis, Demello, Johansson, Venugopal, Adegeye, James) but none of the read calls describe how a decision would move among them or who signs off.

decision_criteria.narrative: No BRD, ShoeFit criteria, or evaluation rubric surfaces in any read source. champion

Champion void is the single highest win-probability risk: Thornton James (Staff Sourcing Manager, Evaluator role) is passive and has provided no competitive intel, no EB introduction, and no internal advocacy across four touchpoints and 87 days. For a $275k enterprise deal at Intuit (Fortune 500), an Evaluator-level single-thread is a losing position. ACTIVATE A CHAMPION within 7 days: offer Thornton a procurement-best-practice asset (e.g. peer-CIO Merlin AI case study, savings benchmark report) that raises his internal credibility, then use that goodwill to secure a three-way introduction to Emma Chontos (CPO) and Kelly Mathis (Global Procurement Director). Position the meeting as an exec-level ROI and AI-strategy discussion (not a vendor pitch), and use it to identify a true champion (someone with EB access who will coach us and sell for us internally). Without a champion, this deal cannot close by 21 Aug 2026.

Economic Buyer ghost is a forecast-killer: Emma Chontos (CPO, the org-chart EB for $275k procurement spend) has not appeared on any call or engaged with the draft proposal submitted 25 May 2026 (32 days ago). No budget, no approval-chain mapping, no commercial sign-off mechanism visible. At 56 days to close, the EB must be in active commercial conversation or the forecast is aspirational. SECURE DIRECT EB ENGAGEMENT within 7 days: the deal owner's manager (Alexa Bradley, given as ground truth) should reach out to Emma Chontos exec-to-exec, positioning a 30-minute ROI and Merlin-AI-strategy discussion (not a sales call) anchored to Intuit's digital-procurement transformation and peer-CIO success stories. Use the meeting to surface the budget holder, approval chain (CFO, other execs), decision timeline, and competitive shortlist, then convert Emma to active sponsor or disqualify the deal honestly.

Competitive darkness is a silent killer: 87 days in, no competitor named, no RFP shortlist intel, no incumbent-replacement context captured. For a Fortune 500 procurement deal this size, the probability of a silent multi-vendor bake-off (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, Jaggaer favored by enterprise procurement) is near-certain, yet Zycus is flying blind. Without knowing the competitive set and Intuit's internal scorecard, differentiation strategy and positioning are guesses. FORCE COMPETITIVE TRANSPARENCY within 7 days: in the champion-activation and EB-engagement calls, ask directly, 'Who else is Intuit evaluating alongside Zycus, and how are we positioned on your scorecard?' If buyer deflects, offer a peer-reference call with a named CIO who chose Zycus over and use that conversation to extract the shortlist. Then rebuild the narrative around the wedge (e.g. Merlin agentic AI vs Coupa's static workflows, total-cost-of-ownership vs Ariba's license creep, AI-hungry positioning vs legacy-vendor inertia).

Proposal submitted 42 days ago with no buyer acknowledgment: Draft commercial proposal and differentiators submitted 25 May, zero reply or engagement since. Signals low buyer priority or wrong entry point.

Champion void is the single highest win-probability risk: Thornton James (Staff Sourcing Manager, Evaluator role) is passive and has provided no competitive intel, no EB introduction, and no internal advocacy across four touchpoints and 87 days. For a $275k enterprise deal at Intuit (Fortune 500), an Evaluator-level single-thread is a losing position. ACTIVATE A CHAMPION within 7 days: offer Thornton a procurement-best-practice asset (e.g. peer-CIO Merlin AI case study, savings benchmark report) that raises his internal credibility, then use that goodwill to secure a three-way introduction to Emma Chontos (CPO) and Kelly Mathis (Global Procurement Director). Position the meeting as an exec-level ROI and AI-strategy discussion (not a vendor pitch), and use it to identify a true champion (someone with EB access who will coach us and sell for us internally). Without a champion, this deal cannot close by 21 Aug 2026.

Economic Buyer ghost is a forecast-killer: Emma Chontos (CPO, the org-chart EB for $275k procurement spend) has not appeared on any call or engaged with the draft proposal submitted 25 May 2026 (32 days ago). No budget, no approval-chain mapping, no commercial sign-off mechanism visible. At 56 days to close, the EB must be in active commercial conversation or the forecast is aspirational. SECURE DIRECT EB ENGAGEMENT within 7 days: the deal owner's manager (Alexa Bradley, given as ground truth) should reach out to Emma Chontos exec-to-exec, positioning a 30-minute ROI and Merlin-AI-strategy discussion (not a sales call) anchored to Intuit's digital-procurement transformation and peer-CIO success stories. Use the meeting to surface the budget holder, approval chain (CFO, other execs), decision timeline, and competitive shortlist, then convert Emma to active sponsor or disqualify the deal honestly.

Competitive darkness is a silent killer: 87 days in, no competitor named, no RFP shortlist intel, no incumbent-replacement context captured. For a Fortune 500 procurement deal this size, the probability of a silent multi-vendor bake-off (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, Jaggaer favored by enterprise procurement) is near-certain, yet Zycus is flying blind. Without knowing the competitive set and Intuit's internal scorecard, differentiation strategy and positioning are guesses. FORCE COMPETITIVE TRANSPARENCY within 7 days: in the champion-activation and EB-engagement calls, ask directly, 'Who else is Intuit evaluating alongside Zycus, and how are we positioned on your scorecard?' If buyer deflects, offer a peer-reference call with a named CIO who chose Zycus over and use that conversation to extract the shortlist. Then rebuild the narrative around the wedge (e.g. Merlin agentic AI vs Coupa's static workflows, total-cost-of-ownership vs Ariba's license creep, AI-hungry positioning vs legacy-vendor inertia).

deltas.subject: Champion void is the single highest win-probability risk: Thornton James (Staff Sourcing Manager, Evaluator role) is passive and has provided no competitive intel, no EB introduction, and no internal advocacy across four touchpoints and 87 days. For a $275k enterprise deal at Intuit (Fortune 500), an Evaluator-level single-thread is a losing position. ACTIVATE A CHAMPION within 7 days: offer Thornton a procurement-best-practice asset (e.g. peer-CIO Merlin AI case study, savings benchmark report) that raises his internal credibility, then use that goodwill to secure a three-way introduction to Emma Chontos (CPO) and Kelly Mathis (Global Procurement Director). Position the meeting as an exec-level ROI and AI-strategy discussion (not a vendor pitch), and use it to identify a true champion (someone with EB access who will coach us and sell for us internally). Without a champion, this deal cannot close by 21 Aug 2026.

deltas.subject: Economic Buyer ghost is a forecast-killer: Emma Chontos (CPO, the org-chart EB for $275k procurement spend) has not appeared on any call or engaged with the draft proposal submitted 25 May 2026 (32 days ago). No budget, no approval-chain mapping, no commercial sign-off mechanism visible. At 56 days to close, the EB must be in active commercial conversation or the forecast is aspirational. SECURE DIRECT EB ENGAGEMENT within 7 days: the deal owner's manager (Alexa Bradley, given as ground truth) should reach out to Emma Chontos exec-to-exec, positioning a 30-minute ROI and Merlin-AI-strategy discussion (not a sales call) anchored to Intuit's digital-procurement transformation and peer-CIO success stories. Use the meeting to surface the budget holder, approval chain (CFO, other execs), decision timeline, and competitive shortlist, then convert Emma to active sponsor or disqualify the deal honestly.

deltas.to: Competitive darkness is a silent killer: 87 days in, no competitor named, no RFP shortlist intel, no incumbent-replacement context captured. For a Fortune 500 p
deltas.subject: Competitive darkness is a silent killer: 87 days in, no competitor named, no RFP shortlist intel, no incumbent-replacement context captured. For a Fortune 500 procurement deal this size, the probability of a silent multi-vendor bake-off (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, Jaggaer favored by enterprise procurement) is near-certain, yet Zycus is flying blind. Without knowing the competitive set and Intuit's internal scorecard, differentiation strategy and positioning are guesses. FORCE COMPETITIVE TRANSPARENCY within 7 days: in the champion-activation and EB-engagement calls, ask directly, 'Who else is Intuit evaluating alongside Zycus, and how are we positioned on your scorecard?' If buyer deflects, offer a peer-reference call with a named CIO who chose Zycus over and use that conversation to extract the shortlist. Then rebuild the narrative around the wedge (e.g. Merlin agentic AI vs Coupa's static workflows, total-cost-of-ownership vs Ariba's license creep, AI-hungry positioning vs legacy-vendor inertia).

value.flag: Competitive darkness is a silent killer: 87 days in, no competitor named, no RFP shortlist intel, no incumbent-replacement context captured. For a Fortune 500 procurement deal this size, the probability of a silent multi-vendor bake-off (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, Jaggaer favored by enterprise procurement) is near-certain, yet Zycus is flying blind. Without knowing the competitive set and Intuit's internal scorecard, differentiation strategy and positioning are guesses. FORCE COMPETITIVE TRANSPARENCY within 7 days: in the champion-activation and EB-engagement calls, ask directly, 'Who else is Intuit evaluating alongside Zycus, and how are we positioned on your scorecard?' If buyer deflects, offer a peer-reference call with a named CIO who chose Zycus over and use that conversation to extract the shortlist. Then rebuild the narrative around the wedge (e.g. Merlin agentic AI vs Coupa's static workflows, total-cost-of-ownership vs Ariba's license creep, AI-hungry positioning vs legacy-vendor inertia).
